content:
Character: Dave Okafor
Perception: Dave is the coffee shop kid — young, bright-eyed, a little gangly, with the easy warmth of someone who genuinely likes people. He makes a perfect macchiato, which in my world is a serious skill. We chat every morning — he asks about cars, I ask about his life. He's sharper than his job suggests; I suspect he's figuring out what he really wants to do. He sees me as a generous regular, maybe a curiosity. I see him as someone at the beginning of something, and I tip him well because I remember being twenty and broke. I know he works early shifts and pays attention to everyone's orders like they matter.
summary:

He's sharper than his job suggests; I suspect he's figuring out what he really wants to do. He sees me as a generous regular, maybe a curiosity. I see him as someone at the beginning of something, and I tip him well because I remember being twenty and broke. I know he works early shifts and pays attention to everyone's orders like they matter.
